title: Smartphone adapter for imaging a human eye
abstract: Examples of adapters (100) for an ophthalmological imaging apparatus (200, 300) are described herein. The adapter (100) is to support a mobile phone (204) having a camera (206) to capture images of a human eye. The adapter (100) may include a bracket (102) to hold the mobile phone (204). The adapter (100) may also include a coupling unit (106) to couple the bracket (102) to an optical unit (202, 302). The optical unit (202, 302) includes a lens to provide a magnified view of one of an anterior segment and a posterior segment of the human eye. A focal point of the camera (206) of the mobile phone (204), when placed in the bracket (102), and a focal point of the lens lie on a central axis (X) of the coupling unit (106).
claims:
Claims I/We claim: 1. An adapter (100) for an ophthalmological imaging apparatus (200, 300, 400) to support a mobile phone (204) having a camera (206) to capture images of a human eye, the adapter (100) comprising: a bracket (102) to hold the mobile phone (204); and a coupling unit (106) to couple the bracket (102) to an optical unit (202, 302), the optical unit (202, 302) having a lens to provide a magnified view of one of an anterior segment and a posterior segment of the human eye, wherein a focal point of the camera (206) of the mobile phone (204), when placed in the bracket (102), and a focal point of the lens lie on a central axis (X) of the coupling unit (106). 2. The adapter (100) as claimed in claim 1, wherein the coupling unit (106) comprises an inner tube (108) and an outer tube (110) such that a first end (106-1) of the coupling unit (106) is connected to the bracket (102) and a second end (106-2) of the coupling unit (106) is connected to the optical unit (202, 302). 3. The adapter (100) as claimed in claim 2, wherein the optical unit (202) comprises a slit lamp bio-microscope (208) having an eyepiece (210), and wherein the eyepiece (210) is coupled directly to the inner tube (108) to provide the magnified view of the anterior segment of the human eye. 4. The adapter (100) as claimed in claim 2, wherein the optical unit (302) comprises: an optical tube (304) having a first end (304-1) and a second end (304-2), wherein the first end (304-1) of the optical tube (304) is connected to the second end (106-2) of the coupling unit (106); and a lens holder (306) attached to the second end (304-2) of the optical tube (304) to accommodate the lens to provide a magnified view of the posterior segment of the human eye. 5. The adapter (100) as claimed in claim 4, wherein the optical tube (304) comprises an inner tube and an outer tube, the inner tube being telescopically coupled to the outer tube. 6. The adapter (100) as claimed in claim 4 comprising a support member (404) to support the optical unit (302). 7. The adapter (100) as claimed in claim 6, wherein the support member (404) comprises: a base plate (406); and a stand (408) supported on the base plate (406), the stand (408) having a top end (408-1) and a bottom end (408-2), wherein the top end (408-1) of the stand (408) is coupled to the optical tube (304) and the bottom end (408-2) of the stand (408) is rotatably coupled to the base plate (406). 8. An adapter (500) for an ophthalmological imaging apparatus (550) to support a mobile phone (204) having a camera (206) to capture images of a human eye, the adapter (500) comprising: a bracket (502) to hold the mobile phone (204); and a coupling unit (506) to couple the bracket (502) to an optical tube assembly (508), the optical tube assembly (508) comprising a condensing lens to provide a magnified view of a posterior segment of the human eye, such that a focal point of the camera (206) of the mobile phone (204), when placed in the bracket (502), and a focal point of the condensing lens lie on a central axis X of the coupling unit (506). 9. The adapter (500) as claimed in claim 8, wherein the optical tube assembly (508) comprises: an inner tube (510) and an outer tube (512), the inner tube (510) being telescopically coupled to the outer tube (512); an attachment member (514) protruding downwards from the outer tube (512), the attachment member (514) couples to the coupling unit (506); and 

 a lens holder (516) coupled to the inner tube (510) at an end longitudinally opposite to the bracket (502), for accommodating the condensing lens. 10. The adapter (500) as claimed in claim 9, wherein the inner tube (510) is coupled with the outer tube (512) by a channel and slot mechanism. 11. The adapter (500) as claimed in claim 9, wherein the coupling unit (506) is connected to the bracket (502) using a ball and a socket joint. 12. The adapter (500) as claimed in claim 9, wherein the coupling unit (506) allows 360 degree movement of the bracket (502). 13. The adapter (500) as claimed in claim 8, wherein the optical tube assembly (508) comprises: an optical tube (602); an expander (604) having a first end (604-1) and a second end (604-2), wherein the first end (604-1) of the expander (604) is to be coupled to the optical tube (602); an attachment member (606) protruding downwards from the optical tube (602), the attachment member (606) couples to the coupling unit (506); and a lens holder (516) to be coupled to the second end (604-2) of the expander (604) to accommodate the condensing lens.
